briefing.headline: HAL bounced +3.4% today ($40.51). Option last=$0.30 but bid/ask still $0.
briefing.body: HAL recovered from yesterday's $39.18 low back to $40.51 (+$1.33). The option last price doubled from $0.15 → $0.30, but bid=0 ask=0 means no active buyers in the market right now. HAL still needs to reach $43 — another $2.50 rally (+6%) in 9 days. Screener found no new trades. WTI crude at $89.55, down from $91 yesterday. The bounce is encouraging but the trade is still deeply OTM. Watch HAL closely today — if it pushes toward $41.50+ with volume, option buyers may return.
briefing.action_detail: Hold for now. If HAL hits $41.50+ and bid comes back above $0.05, consider selling to recover something. If it fades back below $40, close at any price.

watchlist.name: Factorial Energy
watchlist.thesis: Solid-state battery company. Strong macro tailwinds: Iran/Hormuz crisis driving $5 gas, EV adoption urgency, defense/drone demand (IQT-backed). Market growing 43% CAGR. Risk: pre-scale SPAC, thin volume (~10K shares/day). Strategy: observe first hour daily, consider long on morning dip if volume picks up. Do NOT short — macro tailwinds too strong.
watchlist.watch_for: Volume spike above 50K/day, price stabilization above $15, options chain availability
watchlist.day_trade_plan: Buy long on morning dip (first 30-60 min), $100 position, limit sell at +15-20%, same-day exit. Wait for bid/ask spread to tighten before entering.
